title: Anthropic prompt caching behind a default-on flag
description: Add ephemeral cache_control breakpoints to the AnthropicAdapter using the system-and-last-3 strategy, gated by a promptCaching option that the factory resolves from per-role providerConfig and defaults to enabled for the anthropic provider. When the flag is off, the request payload matches the previous plain-string-system shape. New unit tests capture the mocked Anthropic SDK request to assert both the enabled and disabled paths, and all existing provider suites keep passing.

successCriteria.description: When promptCaching is enabled, chat() passes the system prompt to the Anthropic SDK as a content-block array whose text block carries cache_control of type ephemeral, asserted by a unit test capturing the messages.create arguments.

When promptCaching is enabled and the message history has two or more entries, a cache_control breakpoint is attached to the latest message content and the request never contains more than four cache_control breakpoints in total.
When promptCaching is disabled, chat() sends the system prompt as a plain string and no system block, message block, or tool entry in the captured request carries a cache_control field.

createClient reads the promptCaching value from the role providerConfig, defaults it to enabled for the anthropic provider, and forwards it into the AnthropicAdapter constructor, asserted by a factory unit test.
The AnthropicAdapter constructor accepts an optional options object carrying promptCaching, and the package typechecks with no new TypeScript errors via npm run typecheck.
npm run build completes with no errors after the adapter and factory changes, producing an updated dist for the npm-linked cockpit to consume.

Running npm run test introduces no NEW failures beyond the two documented pre-existing provider-unrelated failures (external-server-graph 37-tool-count and disk.test approval-poll); the new anthropic adapter tests pass and the openai, google, openai-compat, and factory suites stay green.

nonGoals: Do not add cache_control to the OpenAI, Google, or OpenAI-compat adapters; caching stays confined to the Anthropic adapter.
Do not extend ChatResponse.usage with cache token fields or emit cache metrics to telemetry; metrics surfacing is a separate deferred feature.
Do not call the live Anthropic API in tests; assert request shape against a mocked SDK client only.
Do not change the default model, max_tokens value, tool conversion, or response normalization of the adapter.
Do not modify the cockpit or any consumer repository; the only deliverable boundary is the rebuilt agent-bober dist.
stopConditions: Stop when typecheck, lint, build, and unit-test all pass and the new Anthropic adapter tests cover both the enabled and disabled flag paths.
Stop when cache_control references exist only in src/providers/anthropic.ts and its test file, plus the flag-resolution lines in src/providers/factory.ts.
Stop when the disabled-flag request payload matches the previous plain-string-system shape with zero cache_control fields.
definitionOfDone: The Anthropic adapter attaches ephemeral cache_control breakpoints to the system prompt and the most recent message(s) using the system-and-last-3 strategy, capped at four breakpoints per request, whenever the resolved promptCaching flag is enabled (the default for Anthropic). When the flag is disabled the adapter sends a plain-string system and no cache_control anywhere, matching the previous payload shape. The factory reads promptCaching from the role providerConfig and forwards it to the constructor. Every non-Anthropic provider path stays untouched, the project typechecks, lints, and builds, and the full provider unit-test suite passes including new tests that capture the mocked SDK request for both flag states.
assumptions: @anthropic-ai/sdk in package-lock supports cache_control ephemeral on system and message content blocks.
promptCaching is a free-form key in the existing providerConfig record, so no config-schema migration is needed.
The factory BOBER_TEST_DETERMINISTIC stub short-circuits createClient, so payload-shape tests construct or mock the adapter directly rather than relying on that env path.
outOfScope: Cache metrics in usage / telemetry / cockpit
generatorNotes: INSERTION POINTS: src/providers/anthropic.ts AnthropicAdapter constructor (~line 133) and chat() (~line 140); src/providers/factory.ts anthropic case (lines ~159-161). FLAG PLUMBING: in factory.ts extract `const promptCaching = typeof providerConfig?.['promptCaching'] === 'boolean' ? providerConfig['promptCaching'] : true;` and call `new AnthropicAdapter(apiKey, { promptCaching })`. Add an optional 2nd constructor arg `opts?: { promptCaching?: boolean }` (default true) stored on the instance. SDK DETAILS: Anthropic `system` accepts `string | TextBlockParam[]`; to cache the system prompt build `[{ type: 'text', text: system, cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' } }]`. cache_control may also be attached to the LAST content block of a message param (text, tool_use, or tool_result blocks all accept it). STRATEGY (system-and-last-3): one breakpoint on the system block + a breakpoint on the final content block of up to the last 3 messages, but Anthropic permits at most 4 cache_control blocks per request, so cap the total at 4 (system counts as 1 -> at most 3 message breakpoints). A robust minimal implementation: always cache system, plus cache the final content block of the single most-recent message; optionally extend toward last-3 while honoring the cap. GOTCHA: `toAnthropicMessage` returns MessageParam whose content may be a plain string for TextMessage — to attach cache_control you must convert string content into `[{ type: 'text', text, cache_control }]`. Keep model/max_tokens/tool conversion/response normalization byte-identical. TESTING: add src/providers/anthropic.test.ts; use vitest `vi.mock('@anthropic-ai/sdk')` to replace the SDK and capture the object passed to `messages.create`, then assert payload shape for flag on/off. Mirror the structure of openai.test.ts / google.test.ts. For the factory test (C4), assert the flag-resolution logic without setting BOBER_TEST_DETERMINISTIC (which would short-circuit to the stub). CROSS-REFERENCE: the local `claude-api` Claude Code skill documents exact Anthropic cache_control breakpoint semantics and TTL — consult it for the breakpoint placement rather than guessing. This feature is borrow #1 from openhands-bober/.bober/research/research-20260529-borrow-from-hermes-agent.md (hermes-agent agent/prompt_caching.py 'system_and_3').
evaluatorNotes: Verify C1 by inspecting the captured messages.create payload: system must be an array with a text block bearing cache_control.type==='ephemeral'. C2: with >=2 messages, the latest message's final content block carries cache_control AND count every cache_control across system+messages+tools — it must be <= 4. C3 (most important regression guard): with the flag off, system must be a plain string and a deep scan of the request must find zero cache_control fields anywhere. C4: confirm factory defaults promptCaching to true for anthropic and passes it through; also confirm an explicit `providerConfig.promptCaching: false` is honored. C5/C6: run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` — both must exit 0. C7: run `npm run test` (and `npm run lint`) — all pre-existing provider suites must still pass. ISOLATION CHECK: `grep -rn cache_control src/providers` must only match anthropic.ts and anthropic.test.ts. Confirm openai.ts, google.ts, openai-compat.ts are unchanged. BASELINE (captured 2026-05-29 before this sprint): typecheck/lint/build all PASS; `npm run test` had 2 PRE-EXISTING failures UNRELATED to providers — (1) tests/mcp/external-server-graph.test.ts 'registerAllTools registers exactly 37 tools' and (2) src/orchestrator/checkpoints/mechanisms/disk.test.ts 'deletes pending file after approval' (both ~5s poll/timeout-style). For C7, treat ONLY these two as the allowed pre-existing baseline; any additional failure, or any failure inside src/providers/, is a real regression. Do not blame the generator for these two.
